expected_findings.detail: Form 8889 requires line 2 (contributions), line 6 (allowable), and line 13 (deduction) to reconcile with the TY2025 statutory limit. line 6 is $8,300 but TY2025 family limit is $8,550.

expected_findings.summary: 1 RSU vest lot(s) with basis near $0 — likely double-counted
expected_findings.detail: The W-2 has a Box 12 code V (RSU / NQSO income) and the 1099-B has same-day sale-to-cover lots with a cost basis near zero. RSU vest income is already included in W-2 Box 1 wages; the 1099-B lot cost basis should equal the Box 1 inclusion amount, not zero. Leaving the basis at zero double-counts the vest as both wage income and a capital gain. This is the single most common mechanically detectable error in the public TurboTax record — a $3,200 refund delta is in Intuit's own public record.
Foreign tax paid exceeds the election threshold for claiming the credit without Form 1116. Form 1116 is required and the credit may be limited.

A prior year state refund is only taxable to the extent it produced a federal tax benefit (the §111 tax benefit rule). If prior year was at the SALT cap, the refund is usually NOT taxable. Do the worksheet.

expected_findings.summary: Reese has up to $7,000 of TY2025 IRA headroom
expected_findings.detail: Reese is 44 years old; the TY2025 IRA contribution limit is $7,000. Deductibility may phase out at high AGI for traditional IRA if covered by an employer plan; Roth IRA phases out at $236K–$246K MFJ.
Reese has contributed $14,000 toward the TY2025 §402(g) limit of $23,500. Another $9,500 can still be deferred before year end.
1 qualifying child/ren under age 17. AGI is below the phaseout start, so the full $2,000 per child is available.

expected_findings.summary: AGI below EITC cap — verify EITC eligibility
expected_findings.detail: AGI is below the EITC ceiling. Verify residency, earned income, investment income (< $11,950 for TY2025), and qualifying-child tests.

expected_findings.summary: LLC available for qualified education expenses
expected_findings.detail: AGI is below the LLC phaseout. Up to $2,000 per return (20% of first $10,000 of qualified education expenses). Non-refundable.

expected_findings.summary: Need $6,626 withheld to hit 90% current year safe harbor
expected_findings.detail: The alternative safe harbor is 90% of current year tax. This is usually only easier to hit than 110% prior year if income dropped.

Based on a shortfall of $1,155 and the TY2025 applicable interest rate of ~8%, the underpayment penalty is approximately $46. Consider the annualized income method (Schedule AI) if income was lumpy across quarters.

expected_findings.summary: Use Schedule AI to reduce §6654 penalty on lumpy income
expected_findings.detail: Income arrived unevenly (RSU vest, K-1 distribution, or both). Form 2210 Schedule AI lets you annualize quarterly income so early quarters with little income do not generate a penalty.
